Green Growth and Resilience in Massachusetts
events.description: This high-level briefing presents new findings from multi-year research on climate resilience and green economic development across Massachusetts municipalities. The work is based on three surveys conducted in partnership with the Massachusetts Municipal Association (MMA), with valuable input from the Municipal Vulnerability Preparedness (MVP) Program and the Executive Office of Energy and Environmental Affairs (EEA). The event brings together state agencies, municipal leaders, and research partners to discuss workforce development, green job creation, and policy tools to support local impleme

events.title: Introduction to Restorative and Transformative Justice: Seeding Restorative Mindset
events.description: Introduction to Restorative and Transformative Justice: Seeding Restorative Mindset in the UMass Amherst Community In a dominant culture increasingly defined by dynamics of ideological polarization, emotionally-armored conflict, inter- and intra-community call-outs and cancellation campaigns, Restorative and Transformative Justice can offer alternate paths toward principled struggle, intentional repair, collective healing, and coalition building. In this interactive workshop, participants will be invited to root into their own unique core values while learning about the historical origins of r

Orchard Hill Habitat Restoration Volunteer Day
events.description: The UMass Bee Campus Committee needs your help making pollinator habitat on Orchard Hill! Please join us for our second invasive species pull day. This will be a fun chance to learn how to identify invasive plants on campus and help us prepare a site for pollinator habitat restoration! We will end the day with a short pollinator tour on Orchard Hill. What to bring: Wear comfortable clothes, long pants, and closed toed shoes with a good tread that you don't mind getting dirty pulling invasive plants. events.title: Memory, Archives, and Commemoration: Remembering the Soweto Uprising of 16 June, 1976
events.venue: Library, W. E. B. Du Bois
events.description: Livestream link here. Much of our work as librarians, as library staff, as archivists, and as educators, is to work to hold and store memory. We work to facilitate a remembering, whether our own or that of another place or time. Our work as people extends beyond that too; we are storytellers. Some of us carry these stories in our minds, some in our art - poetry, fiction, photographs, oral history, or on our bodies. On 16 June 1976, 50 years ago, and in the days immediately after, between 600 and 1000 people were killed by the South African apartheid state. On 16 June 2026, we come together to

events.title: Kids Like Me
events.description: Meet Oliver, a 12-year-old murder-mystery buff who is growing up with a rare genetic condition. Together with his family, they reframe what it means to live with disabilities, while embarking on an exciting adventure to create a murder-mystery caper. Join us for a sneak preview of this new doc, partially filmed in Amherst, MA. Post-film Q&A with filmmaker Cynthia Lowen. Free to Amherst Cinema Members.

events.title: Turner & Constable
events.description: Two of Britain’s greatest painters, J.M.W. Turner and John Constable were also the greatest of rivals. Celebrating the 250th anniversary of their births, with Turner born in 1775 and Constable in 1776, this film explores their lives and legacies alongside a groundbreaking Tate Britain exhibition.

events.title: Something's Gotta Give
events.description: An aging music mogul (Jack Nicholson) falls for his young girlfriend’s mother (Diane Keaton) after a heart attack forces him to stay at her Hamptons beach house. Cited by Keaton as a personal favorite within her filmography, this rom-com earned her a fourth Oscar nomination.

events.title: Sherman's March
events.description: Armed with a 16mm camera and a grant to make a documentary about Sherman’s 1864 march to the sea, Ross McElwee gets sidetracked, shifting his attention from the historical to the personal and the battlefield of modern love. This landmark of first-person filmmaking is newly re-released in a 4K restoration. Director Ross McElwee joins us for a post-screening conversation. Free to Amherst Cinema Members.

events.title: NEPM Book Club: 'Seek Immediate Shelter' with author Vincent Yu
events.description: Join the NEPM Book Club Thursday, July 23 at 7 p.m. for a Q&A with author Vincent Yu about his debut novel, "Seek Immediate Shelter."

events.title: Whale Rider
events.description: A young Maori girl fights to fulfill a destiny her grandfather refuses to recognize in this contemporary story of love, rejection, and triumph.

events.title: All the President's Men
events.description: Robert Redford and Dustin Hoffman star as Washington Post reporters Bob Woodward and Carl Bernstein in this ripped-from-the-headlines political thriller, one of the great movies about journalism and winner of four Oscars.

events.title: Volver
events.description: Equal parts tragedy, melodrama, and farce, this story of three generations of working-class Spanish women is a comedic and compassionate tribute to resilience in the face of life’s most outrageous tribulations. Penélope Cruz delivers an Oscar-nominated performance in Almodóvar’s modern masterpiece.

events.title: The Making of Casablanca: Film School in 60 Minutes
events.description: If you don’t know a pan from a dolly, or an over-the-shoulder from a two-shot, this program is for you! Local filmmaker and film expert Nina Kleinberg walks us through the making of CASABLANCA, followed by a screening of the film.

events.title: When Harry Met Sally...
events.description: With an Oscar-nominated script by Nora Ephron, this foundational rom-com follows friends Harry Burns (Billy Crystal) and Sally Albright (Meg Ryan) as they attempt to navigate their way around romantic entanglement.

events.title: When Harry Met Sally...
events.description: With an Oscar-nominated script by Nora Ephron, this foundational rom-com follows friends Harry Burns (Billy Crystal) and Sally Albright (Meg Ryan) as they attempt to navigate their way around romantic entanglement.

events.title: A Body To Live In
events.description: A BODY TO LIVE IN offers an uncompromising look at the rise of BDSM performance art, body modification and the “modern primitives” cultural movement through the agonies and ecstasies of transgressive artist Fakir Musafar and the communities that surrounded him. Director Angelo Madsen joins us for a post-film discussion. Free to Amherst Cinema Members.

events.title: City of God
events.description: In the favelas of 1970s Rio de Janeiro, two young men choose different paths. Rocket is a budding photographer, while Zé is an ambitious drug dealer diving into a dangerous life of crime.

events.title: Awake, My Soul: The Story of the Sacred Harp
events.description: Explore the history, music, and traditions of Sacred Harp singing, which has survived over 200 years tucked away from sight in the rural deep South. This haunting, moving music lives on today at community singing events. Post-film Q&A with filmmaker Matt Hinton.
